themes.exampleQuote: A perfect speakeasy vibe with the best cocktails I've had in Pittsburgh.
The drink is a superb riff on a Sazerac/Manhattan/old fashioned. Slightly sweet, little liquorice flavor, booze heavy.
Alicia was one of the best bartenders I've had, definitely deserves the bar manager position.
This policy is only mentioned in the menu and in very small print.
I have tried to get in a few times but it is always super packed!

notable_quote: I will be making the drive from Cleveland JUST for this bar in the near future.
sentiment_summary: Reviewers are most energized by the moody botanical interior, the bartender-driven cocktail experience, and the general sense of stumbling onto something Pittsburgh hasn't fully claimed yet; the recurring friction is the 90-minute table policy, which feels punitive when servers execute it without warning.
quarter_narrative: Twenty-eight Pittsburgh creators posted more than thirty TikToks at Commerce Bar this quarter, generating over 1.6 million plays, while Commerce Bar posted a single Instagram photo and no reels of its own. The top video alone, @andrewgardone calling it 'slept on' with 443,000 plays, did more positioning work than anything the bar produced itself. Hidden Harbor leads the Pittsburgh Bars family this issue on the strength of social momentum, a signal Commerce Bar clearly has the raw material to compete on, given the creator activity already swirling around it. Moving up from second requires the bar to show up in the same spaces where the city is already talking about it.
tldr_read: A TikTok magnet with 1.6 million plays and a dormant Instagram to match; Neighborhood Staples, number two in Pittsburgh Bars.
tldr_meaning: The creator signal is already outsized for this tier; the rank ceiling is determined by whether the bar's own digital presence ever catches up to what the city is producing on its behalf.

diagnosis_pullquote.line: Second In Pittsburgh Bars, Carried By 1.6 Million Creator Plays. The Bar Hasn't Answered.

playbook.headline: Build a basic Google presence immediately
playbook.action: Commerce Bar has no website and no phone number on Google, while the family median conversion path is a perfect score; add both this week.

playbook.headline: Post reels before the creator heat cools
playbook.action: Zero reels in the last 30 days against 28 outside creators actively filming; one bartender-narrated cocktail reel per week would double the current output immediately.

playbook.headline: Address the time-limit policy on-camera
playbook.action: The 90-minute cap is the only recurring negative in reviews; a short, direct video explaining it turns a trust gap into a transparency signal before guests arrive.
